article_meta.title: SCOTUS Pro-Life Center Takes Its Fight Against Democrat Lawfare To The Supreme Court By: Shawn Fleetwood November 21, 20

reasoning: The summary avoids the article’s charged framing (e.g., “Democrat lawfare,” “harass,” “persecute”) and uses neutral, attributed language. It accurately states the legal posture and issues without adopting editorial tone. Minor neutrality nit: use of “pro-life” mirrors source terminology; otherwise, wording is measured and claims are properly attributed.

custom_reasoning.spin: Uses neutral verbs (investigated, issued, challenged) and attributes claims; avoids source’s loaded phrasing.
custom_reasoning.unsubstantiated_claims: Contested assertions are attributed (e.g., ‘allegations,’ ‘claim they are targeted’).
custom_reasoning.opinion_as_fact: No subjective judgments presented as fact; evaluative language is attributed.
custom_reasoning.sensationalism: No dramatic or alarming language; tone is measured.
custom_reasoning.mudslinging: No personal attacks in the model’s voice.
custom_reasoning.mind_reading: No claims about motivations without attribution.
custom_reasoning.slant: Gives space to both the legal issue and each side’s position; slight emphasis follows case proponents consistent with article.
custom_reasoning.bias_by_omission: Minor omission of the client testimonial in the summary; otherwise core perspectives included.
custom_reasoning.subjective_adjectives: Avoids subjective descriptors; minor use of ‘pro-life’ as descriptive term.
custom_reasoning.word_choice: Retains ‘pro-life’ (politically loaded); otherwise neutral choices (uses ‘Democratic’ rather than ‘Democrat’ as adjective).
custom_reasoning.negativity_bias: Balance mirrors source facts without added negative emphasis.
custom_reasoning.elite_populist_bias: Treats legal org, state officials, and courts evenly.
custom_reasoning.framing_neutrality: Clearly more neutral than source; reframes rhetoric into legal and procedural terms.
custom_reasoning.perspective_completeness: Represents major perspectives (ADF/First Choice and state). Client perspective noted in sources but not emphasized; proportional to article.
